refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions. Verify compliance with safety plan via incident/near-miss logs and safety audit checklist.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Tele-operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle via Teleoperation with sufficient latency, fidelity, and safety interlocks to achieve productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services shall continuously monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (worksite plan, operational policies) without preventing achievement of productivity_target under safe conditions.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with dual control architecture (on-site joystick + remote teleoperation interface) and real-time telemetry feedback to operator(s), supported by autonomous safety monitoring that enforces geofence and load-limit constraints.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable bucket/attachment configurations, operator-selectable task profiles (dig, load, transport, dump), and adaptive control algorithms that optimize cycle time based on soil conditions and operator input.

DP-C: Implement a closed-loop productivity tracking system that measures actual output (m³/h or ton/h) in real-time, compares against productivity_target, and provides operator feedback (alerts, recommendations) to guide task execution and identify bottlenecks.

DP-D: Establish a hierarchical safety architecture with Safety Services as the authoritative constraint enforcer, receiving telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation, and issuing SafetyAlert commands that override or inhibit unsafe operator commands.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A + DP-D (integrated): Integrate a high-capacity hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with dual control architecture (on-site joystick + remote teleoperation interface) and real-time telemetry feedback, supported by a hierarchical Safety Services layer that continuously monitors operational state and enforces safety constraints via SafetyAlert commands. This combination directly addresses the refined FR by enabling both on-site and remote operation modes while maintaining safety as a non-negotiable constraint.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical capability (bucket capacity, hydraulic power, cycle time) is independent of control architecture (on-site vs. remote).
Teleoperation introduces latency and fidelity dependencies that may couple with operator skill and task complexity; mitigated by Safety Services constraint enforcement.
Safety Services constraint enforcement (geofence, load limits) may couple with productivity if constraints are overly conservative; requires careful tuning during design refinement.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP avoids tight coupling by separating concerns: Vehicle provides mechanical capability, dual control provides operator interface flexibility, and Safety Services provides constraint enforcement. Latency and fidelity are managed through explicit interface design (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) rather than implicit assumptions.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic technology and operator skill levels (on-site and remote).
ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies can be formalized as computable constraints (geofence, load limits, speed limits, etc.) that Safety Services can enforce.
RISK: Tele-operator latency may degrade productivity below target if network link introduces >500ms round-trip delay; requires network design specification.
RISK: Operator skill variance (on-site vs. remote) may cause productivity to fall below target for less-experienced operators; requires training/proficiency requirements.
RISK: Safety constraint enforcement may inadvertently prevent safe high-productivity operations if constraints are not carefully calibrated; requires iterative tuning.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A + DP-D to explicitly specify: (1) target latency budget for Teleoperation (e.g., <200ms round-trip), (2) formalization of safety constraints as computable rules (geofence polygon, load limits, speed profiles), (3) operator proficiency requirements (training hours, certification), and (4) productivity measurement methodology (sampling interval, aggregation period, environmental normalization).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It is responsible for coordinating dual-mode operation (on-site and remote), ensuring all external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are properly connected, and delivering the integrated system to the customer for productivity evaluation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load, hydraulic pressure, cycle status) streamed from Vehicle to Teleoperation and Safety Services. Enables remote operator situational awareness and safety monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (on-site joystick or remote interface) received by Vehicle control system. Specifies desired bucket motion, vehicle movement, and task mode (dig, load, transport, dump).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services issues constraint-enforcement commands (inhibit, override, alert) to Vehicle and Teleoperation based on real-time monitoring. Ensures worksite safety plan and operational policies are enforced.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel between on-site Vehicle and remote Tele-operator. Carries Tasking commands (remote -> Vehicle) and Telemetry feedback (Vehicle -> remote). Subject to latency and bandwidth constraints.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for this FR. It owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and is responsible for delivering the complete system to the customer. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns mechanical capability and on-site control, but does not own Teleoperation or Safety Services. Productivity depends on Vehicle capability, but also on operator effectiveness and safety constraint enforcement. High diffusion: Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces cross the boundary.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and network communication, but does not own Vehicle mechanics or Safety Services. Productivity depends on Teleoperation latency and fidelity, but also on Vehicle capability and safety constraints. High diffusion: Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces cross the boundary.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement logic, but depends on Telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation, and issues SafetyAlert commands to both. Does not own productivity measurement or operator control. High diffusion: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces cross the boundary.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because: (1) it owns the integration of all sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) required to achieve the refined FR, (2) it is responsible for coordinating dual-mode operation and ensuring all interfaces are properly connected, (3) it is the natural delivery unit to the customer for productivity evaluation, and (4) it minimizes diffusion by keeping all critical dependencies within the boundary.
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services are owned by different teams/vendors, integration complexity and interface management may increase. Mitigation: establish clear interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and integration test plan.

RISK: If productivity measurement is not explicitly owned by NGHE Solution, there may be ambiguity about how productivity_target is verified. Mitigation: assign productivity measurement and reporting to NGHE Solution or explicitly delegate to a sub-module with clear acceptance criteria.

RISK: If Safety Services constraint enforcement is too aggressive, it may prevent safe high-productivity operations. Mitigation: require iterative tuning and validation of safety constraints during design refinement and field trials.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly assign ownership of productivity measurement and reporting to NGHE Solution (or a designated sub-module). Define interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with specific data formats, latency budgets, and reliability requirements. Establish integration test plan and field trial protocol to validate dual-mode operation and safety constraint enforcement.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (DP-A + DP-D) directly addresses the refined FR by providing: (1) a high-capacity Vehicle with mechanical capability to achieve productivity_target, (2) dual control architecture (on-site + remote) to support both operator modes, (3) real-time Telemetry feedback for operator situational awareness, and (4) hierarchical Safety Services to enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies. The DP is logically sound and avoids unnecessary complexity.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface carries high-volume real-time data (position, orientation, bucket load, hydraulic pressure, cycle status) that must be streamed reliably to both Teleoperation and Safety Services. Mitigation: specify Telemetry data format, sampling rate, and reliability requirements (e.g., <100ms latency, 99.9% delivery).

SafetyAlert interface is a critical safety-critical command channel that must override or inhibit operator commands. Mitigation: implement SafetyAlert as a high-priority, low-latency channel with explicit acknowledgment and logging.

Network Link introduces latency and bandwidth constraints that may couple with operator skill and task complexity. Mitigation: specify Network Link latency budget (e.g., <200ms round-trip) and bandwidth requirements (e.g., >1 Mbps for Telemetry + Tasking).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution boundary is appropriate because it owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and is responsible for managing all external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No critical dependencies are diffused outside the boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR is feasible via representative operations trials: (1) conduct field trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience), (2) measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target, (3) log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions, (4) verify compliance with safety plan via incident/near-miss logs and safety audit checklist. Acceptance criteria are clear and measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is allocated to NGHE Solution, which owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (mechanical capability), on-site Operator (situational awareness and control), Tele-operator (remote command and monitoring), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP to explicitly specify: (1) target latency budget for Teleoperation (e.g., <200ms round-trip), (2) formalization of safety constraints as computable rules, (3) operator proficiency requirements, and (4) productivity measurement methodology.
Establish interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with specific data formats, latency budgets, and reliability requirements.
Define integration test plan and field trial protocol to validate dual-mode operation, safety constraint enforcement, and productivity achievement.
Assign explicit ownership of productivity measurement and reporting to NGHE Solution or a designated sub-module.

decision.justification: The refined FR is logically sound and the selected DP is appropriate, but the FR is not yet atomic. The refined FR has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) that address distinct concerns: Vehicle mechanical capability, on-site operator effectiveness, tele-operator effectiveness, and Safety Services constraint enforcement. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to identify specific design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to the next level of decomposition will clarify the design and enable more precise allocation of requirements to sub-modules.
decision.justification_detail: The current level (NGHE Solution) is appropriate for top-level system integration, but the sub-FRs require further refinement to be implementable. For example, FR-NGHE-001.1 (Vehicle mechanical capability) must be decomposed into specific requirements for bucket capacity, hydraulic power, cycle time, and control responsiveness. FR-NGHE-001.2 and FR-NGHE-001.3 (operator effectiveness) must be decomposed into requirements for situational awareness (camera views, telemetry display), control responsiveness (joystick sensitivity, command latency), and task proficiency. FR-NGHE-001.4 (Safety Services) must be decomposed into specific safety constraints (geofence, load limits, speed limits) and enforcement mechanisms (inhibit, override, alert).
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Tele-operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle via Teleoperation with sufficient latency, fidelity, and safety interlocks to achieve productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services shall continuously monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (worksite plan, operational policies) without preventing achievement of productivity_target under safe conditions.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic technology and operator skill levels (on-site and remote).
Worksite safety plan and operational policies can be formalized as computable constraints (geofence, load limits, speed limits, etc.) that Safety Services can enforce.
Operator proficiency requirements can be established and validated through training and certification.
Network Link latency can be controlled to <200ms round-trip for remote operation.
Safety constraint enforcement can be tuned to avoid preventing safe high-productivity operations.
open_risks: Tele-operator latency may degrade productivity below target if network link introduces >500ms round-trip delay.
Operator skill variance (on-site vs. remote) may cause productivity to fall below target for less-experienced operators.
Safety constraint enforcement may inadvertently prevent safe high-productivity operations if constraints are not carefully calibrated.
Integration complexity and interface management may increase if Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services are owned by different teams/vendors.
evidence_needed: Field trial data: actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) achieved during representative operations, broken down by operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions.
Safety audit data: incident/near-miss logs, safety constraint violations, and safety audit checklist results.
Network performance data: latency, bandwidth, and reliability measurements for Network Link under representative operating conditions.
Operator proficiency data: training hours, certification results, and productivity correlation with operator experience level.

Design specifications: interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link; safety constraint formalization; operator proficiency requirements; productivity measurement methodology.
